description: Harden the MCP App registry contract: layered tier merge (system identity + user runtime override), structured install error, target-tier install API, and a forward-looking URL template resolver. docxmcp deployment is out of scope; it is the trigger that exposed the registry bugs, not a target of change.

activities.title: Layered merge of MCP App registry
activities.description: Replace system-wins with layered merge: system tier owns identity (path/source/tools/schema), user tier overrides runtime endpoint fields (url, enabled, config).

activities.title: Resolve runtime URL template
activities.description: Expand ${UID}, ${USER}, ${HOME}, ${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR} in the per-user daemon. Closed token set; process.getuid() is the only uid source.

activities.title: Dial MCP App endpoint
activities.description: connectMcpApps() and incoming/dispatcher.ts both pass through the resolver before dialing the Unix socket or HTTP-over-unix endpoint.

activities.title: Install with explicit target tier
activities.description: system-manager_install_mcp_app accepts target: 'system' | 'user'; McpAppStoreError surfaces the underlying cause.

activities.title: Verify docxmcp routes under the new merge
activities.description: docxmcp deployment is out of scope (host-shared `.run/` socket is canonical per docxmcp's own 2026-05-28 decision). This activity verifies the existing user-tier entry routes correctly under the layered merge and that the resolver passes the literal URL through untouched.

arrows.label: merged app entry with templated url
existing docxmcp user-tier entry + live .run/ socket
literal URL routed through resolver untouched
MCP initialize / HTTP upload to docxmcp
